review_text: Terribly bad.First they send me the wrong part with a manufacturing defect, then I have to wait 2 months for the right part which has not yet been sent out from them. I do not recommend anyone to shop at AUTODOC at all.VERY BAD SERVICE!!!____________________________________________________________Got a message from the workshop today that the new part you sent was also the wrong part. They spent 9 working hours trying to adapt it but it was not possible.1/ You have received the original part number.2/ You have received the registration number of the car.3/ You have received the chassis number of the car.4/ You have received pictures of the old part.You have managed to send me the wrong part for the second time.You are completely incompetent at AutodocYou must pay me compensation for the hours that have been spent at the workshop_________________________________________________________Have now sent Autodoc a compensation claim for extra expenses that I incurred from the workshop. I agreed on a fixed price with the workshop of NOK 6,800 to replace the exhaust manifold. The bill from the workshop came to NOK 14,720 because Autodoc sent me a manifold with manufacturing defects twice. The additional cost for me is therefore NOK 7,920, which I believe Autodoc must compensate me for. I have now emailed them all the documentation from the workshop, just need to wait and see what they do with the case.WRITTEN FROM THE WORKSHOP:Complaint about additional costs – incorrect and incomplete parts delivered by AutodocTo: AutodocFrom: Bilsenteret Namsos AS – on behalf of the customerRegarding: Incorrectly delivered/defective exhaust manifolds and resulting additional costsReg. no.: ¤¤¤¤¤¤¤We hereby wish to file a formal complaint about parts delivered by Autodoc, as incorrect and defective exhaust manifolds caused significant extra work and additional costs for the customer.Background of the case The customer ordered an exhaust manifold from Autodoc and delivered the parts to us for installation. The work was agreed to cost NOK 6,800 including VAT. During the work, we documented the following:Work process and deviations related to the parts16.01.2026Dismantling of the exhaust manifoldDismantling of the turboDismantling of the rear engine mountDismantling of the exhaust systemDismantling of the plate under the engineDiscovered that the first exhaust manifold delivered by Autodoc was incorrect, due to a deviation in the EGR connection (wrong widening on the stub)17.02.2026Installation of new exhaust manifold (second part delivered by Autodoc)Turbo could not be installed, as it interfered with the exhaust manifoldDismantling of exhaust manifold againAttempt to install turbo on manifoldNew attempt to install manifold with turboConclusion: Part no. 2 from Autodoc had incorrect dimensions / too thick material, and did not fitThis resulted in the work having to be stopped and redone several times, without progress due to insufficient part quality.23.02.2026Installation of old exhaust manifold (welded and repaired by He-Plan)Installation of turboInstallation of engine mountInstallation of exhaust systemInstallation of plate under engineTest drive – OKCustomer provided own gaskets and mounting hardware Additional costsThe customer initially received a price of NOK 6,800 including VAT for the job.Due to two unusable and incorrectly manufactured exhaust manifolds delivered by Autodoc, the customer ended up with:NOK 12,800 including VAT for laborNOK 1,920 including VAT for welding/repair of the old manifoldTotal additional cost: NOK 7,920 including VATThese are additional costs that would not have occurred if the parts from Autodoc had been correct and mountable.Our assessmentIncorrect and incomplete parts caused:Unnecessary extra workRepeated disassembly and assembly without progressExtra costs for the customerDelay in vehicle repairAll additional costs could have been avoided if the parts had the correct specification and fit.ClaimOn behalf of the customer, we request that Autodoc:Refunds the additional cost of NOK 7,920 including VATOr alternatively, refunds the cost of the incorrectly delivered/incomplete partsDocumentation and work descriptions are attached.ConclusionWe ask for your feedback as soon as possible, as well as information on how this matter is further handled.We are happy to assist with additional documentation if necessary.Sincerely,Bilsenteret Namsos ASOn behalf of the customer
supplier_response: Hello, Thank you for your comment. Indeed, the part you received has a manufacturing defect. A courier will pick it up shortly. We have also sent you the manufacturers' listings of parts that will fit your vehicle. Please take some time to look through the proposal. We recommend waiting until you receive the required spare parts before booking an appointment with a mechanic: https://www.autodoc.co.no/services/frakt Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I entered my vehicle data correctly. According to AUTODOC, the part should have fitted my car, but in reality it did not.The issue was only discovered during engine disassembly. The item is new, unused, and still in its original packaging.Despite this, AUTODOC refused to accept a return because the return period had expired. No goodwill solution was offered, even though the mistake was not mine.Instead of taking responsibility, AUTODOC shifted the issue to a third-party seller.Result: I am left with a loss of €253.50.Very disappointing customer service. If something goes wrong, the customer is left alone.Update:Trotz der öffentlichen Antwort von AUTODOC, dass sich ein Quality-Control-Manager bei mir melden würde, habe ich bis heute keinerlei Rückmeldung erhalten.Seitdem sind mehrere Tage vergangen, ohne Kontaktaufnahme oder Lösungsvorschlag.Die Bewertung bleibt daher aufrecht.
supplier_response: Hello Nazif, We are sorry to hear about such situation. We strongly suggest you always check OEN references in the description of the product. OEN verification gives you an almost 100% guarantee that you are ordering the exact part you need. Our quality control manager will check the case and contact you. Please check your email box and spam box. You were informed that you can send back parts to our warehouse as an exception.  And return label was sent to your email address on 03.02.2026 Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I ordered a SACHS flywheel (2294 501 267) from Autodoc, paying a premium SACHS price.What I received was a broken, recycled LUK flywheel, inside a SACHS box, tied together with zip ties and taped shut. The flywheel had huge play throughout, making it completely unsafe for installation.SACHS/ZF confirmed that this flywheel is not even produced by SACHS, but sourced from LUK for rare VWAG models. Yet Autodoc never disclosed this anywhere, selling it as SACHS at a premium price.The price difference is shocking: in Bulgaria it can be €350–€450, while on other European sites it is only about €150, yet the product inside is identical to a much cheaper LUK part. Autodoc profits massively by misleading customers.This is misleading, unsafe, and completely unacceptable. I returned the part the same day, but Autodoc failed to resolve the issue, leaving my car unusable for 2 months and causing 4 months of delays, frustration, and extra costs.Autodoc must stop misleading customers and clearly disclose when parts are sourced from third parties, especially when they are broken or unsafe.
supplier_response: Hello Kristian Karakashev! We sincerely apologize for the experience you’ve had with the flywheel. We understand your frustration regarding the incorrect part, safety concerns, and the inconvenience caused by the delays. Please be informed that our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to update with your case in detail and assist you further to resolve this matter. Your feedback will also be used to improve our product verification and disclosure processes. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Purchased 4 Bridgestone tyres which said they were in stock, chased after 3 days of them still being sat in order being transferred between warehouses, they told me that there was a delay and on the 4th day completely cancelled the order without and reason. They are still selling the same products are being in stock but for £40 more per tyre, this sounds sus. Think they’ve cancelled the order because they want to get more money for it, it’s been at the price I have paid for them for at least 4 weeks as I have been tracking them. Shame really, happy to take your money under false pretences then cancel without any reason, wasted a week in waiting and now having to sort again. I wouldn’t be using Autodoc again.Update - had a chat with a chap called Andrew from Audodoc and they have explained the situation. Would help if it’s made clear on the site beforehand, but it is what it is now.  Appreciate you explaining things properly. Thanks
supplier_response: Hello Suki Nandhra! Thank you for taking the time for your review. We’re sorry for the inconvenience caused by the stock transfer and the cancellation of your order. Availability and pricing can change if the original supplier runs out of stock and an alternative supplier is found at a different price. We appreciate your understanding after the explanation from our agent and will take your feedback on clearer website communication into account. Thank you for your patience and for giving us the opportunity to clarify the situation. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

supplier_response: Hello!😊 We are very pleased to receive positive feedback from our customers! Good luck on any road. Best regards, AUTODOC Team🛠️

review_text: Very disappointing and misleading experience with AutodocMy experience with Autodoc has been extremely frustrating and disappointing.I ordered spare parts after Autodoc’s website explicitly confirmed compatibility with my vehicle. My VIN number is already saved in my personal account, and based on this information, the website assured that the parts were suitable. However, when the parts arrived, they turned out to be completely incompatible.As a result, I had to return the items and paid shipping costs twice (to and from Geneva), totaling approximately CHF 85. Autodoc refunded only the price of the parts and refused to reimburse the shipping costs, even though the mistake was entirely theirs.Instead of taking responsibility, they attempted to blame the customer by claiming that selecting parts by make and model is “not accurate.” This raises a serious question: why does their website confirm compatibility if it is unreliable?This was not my first bad experience with Autodoc. Previously, I was offered a compensation voucher for a similar mistake, which later turned out to be invalid and unusable. Now they repeat the same approach by offering a small discount coupon instead of refunding the actual costs caused by their error.In summary:Incorrect and incompatible partsCustomer forced to pay for the company’s mistakesUnreliable vouchersAvoidance instead of real solutionsA very poor customer experience. I will not use Autodoc again and cannot recommend them.
supplier_response: Hello Sabah Jassem! We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and frustration you’ve experienced. Please be aware that the parts displayed on our website whether selected through the vehicle selector or by entering your registration number are intended as general recommendations. Because vehicles often have unique specifications beyond just the model and body type, the most accurate way to confirm compatibility is by using the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) or the Original Equipment Number (OEN). Even if your VIN is stored in your account, our system does not automatically verify compatibility against it. This important step must be carried out by our trained specialists to ensure the correct fit. Our customer support team is always available to help with a full compatibility check, as product listings only provide technical details and cannot guarantee suitability for every vehicle. Please rest assured that our Quality Control Manager will be reaching out to you shortly to personally review your case and provide further assistance, ensuring the matter is handled properly. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I ordered a steering rack for my 2009 Audi A4 B8 2.0l TDI (143hp) 2 weeks ago. The delivery was fast, it arrived in only a few days, which i liked. Yet it turns out a part was missing in the delivery: The metal plug. Instead, it was delivered with a plastic one to keep the dust from entering. According to my local mechanic, without the right plug / lock, the plastic one wouldn't keep up with the oil pressure and it would just explode.I have tried to contact AUTODOC in multiple ways.-The AI assistant on their app told me I'd get an e-mail in 24-48 hours, 4 days later I haven't received anything.-I have called their numbers in hope of reaching an actual human instead of an AI, but guess what. Dan the virtual assistant picked up and he was no help at all. I could repeat a sentence 3 times and he wouldn't understand a thing I said, so after some time he gave up and said "We're happy we could solve your problem!"Next I wanted to see if it's possible to atleast return the steering rack, but no, "Unfortunately, this product is not eligible for a refund."I spent a total of 480 bucks on this thing, and for someone at the age of 19, I simply cannot afford this waste of money.And even if this so called "plug" isn't actually included in the product, then at least help me find the missing piece I need.I might not be experienced in cars and I could also be wrong about what I said in the beginning, it might just be called something else or it might just be an entirely other piece that I'm talking about. My main point is that there is literally no way for me to reach out to AUTODOC because of their awfully designed AI assistant.
supplier_response: Hello ! We’re very sorry to read about your experience and understand how frustrating this situation must be, especially given the importance and cost of the part. Regarding your case, we would like to clarify that we have attempted to contact you, however the phone number and email address provided with the order appear to be invalid, which prevents our team from reaching you directly. To properly investigate the missing metal plug, check the product specifications, and offer you the correct solution (replacement part, clarification, or return options), we kindly ask you to contact us directly by email at trust@autodoc.de. Please include clear photos of the received steering rack, and the area where the plug should be installed. Once we receive your email, our support team will review the case manually and assist you as quickly as possible. We truly regret the inconvenience and appreciate your patience. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

Thank you!Biggest Scammers - Stole my FundsMy return was received 5 weeks ago and I ordered replacment product and paid for both. supplier_response: Hello HALI! Thank you for sharing the outcome. We’re glad to hear that the refund has now been processed and our support was able to bring the case to a resolution. We truly regret the inconvenience and the time it took to sort this out, as this is not the experience we want for our customers. Your feedback is appreciated and will help us improve our processes going forward. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: When a car battery dies you need a replacement quickly, and thats what I thought I would get,.....but not with these people. I ordered a new battery on Thursday 11th Dec, in good time for a Christmas getaway. In formation suggested delivery on Monday 15th, but of course no delivery. Now I have no inforamtion about when it will arrive only that it has been collected by the carrier. We are due to leave Sunday, but I have little faith. I shall NEVER use Autodoc again, and I will also make a point of telling all my motoring friends about this dreadful service.....or lack of service!
supplier_response: Hello John! We are sorry for such situation. Our quality control manager contacted you concerning this issue and we hope that solution was found. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I received a faulty air pump which I had a replacement for , when the replacement arrived that was also faulty so I contacted autodoc and one of their agents said I could get a refund if second one is faulty and specifically that there is no need to return the item.  Fast forward  , then they have said I cannot get a refund until I return both items and have to spend my own money on printing the customs form and label pages   to get the refund of £1.85. I have spent over £100 with AUTODOC and even placed another order a few days before this all occurred . The agents are unhelpful and rude. I would never order from here again after this occasion , still have not even received a refund or even a decent replacement air pump which has left me without .. I do not care for the £1.85 refund but the service has disgusted me and how unhelpful they are to returning customers .Please stay clear .
supplier_response: Hello MK! Thank you for sharing your experience, and we’re truly sorry for the inconvenience caused. Receiving two faulty items and then facing conflicting information about the return process is unacceptable, and we fully understand your frustration. This is not the level of service we aim to provide, especially to a loyal customer. Please contact us at trust@autodoc.de so we can review your case in detail, arrange the correct solution, and ensure your refund or replacement is handled properly without any additional hassle. We’re here to help and want to make this right for you. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: 2 out of t: I ordered a set of headlights for my Polo in May after someone smashed them backing into me. I had them fitted with my annual service in October (I am self-employed and didn’t wanna lose an extra day of work and income). Upon fitting the garage realises one of the lights is broken so I issue a warranty claim. They send me a replacement part very quickly- so far, so good.However, the part I was sent is for brakes. So, I send it back using their return label. It arrives on the 24th of Nov (I have proof of tracking). And I wait. But no light. All I get is emails that details are missing. At first it’s my address. Then they say the returned parts haven’t been received. Last week their customer service assures me the case will be escalated and prioritised. Today they email me that they are closing it. More assurances that the warranty department will take care of it. Nobody can tell me what details are missing. There is no contact for the warranty department or an email address to contact them. I’m at a point where I just want a refund for the headlight I paid for.Summary: If nothing goes wrong they are a decent provider of parts. As soon as something goes wrong, it’s shambles and unnerving and either ineptitude or stalling tactics. At the moment, would absolutely not recommend.Update 19/12/2025: Going up from 2 Stars to 3.5After posting my initial review they contacted me and settled he issue swiftly. All resolved but it's still not impressive that it needs a Trustpilot review to get proper help. Will use them again but cautiously. They have to win back full trust. Once that's achieved I'll happily review again.
supplier_response: Hello Corny Alba Sibirica! We are sorry that you have faced inconveniences and we are sorry for the mixed up with your warranty replacement. Our quality control manager will  check your case and contact you in the nearest time to resolve the issue. If you have any questions, please contact us at help@autodoc.de Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Non existant costummer service.  Part arrived, it was a back light. I tried it on the car and it looked different from the original part, the dimentions were slightly off and it was extremely cheaply built. Took it out immediately and went to the website to fill out a from to return it. The website said it was already too late to return which is rediculos because I had only gotten the part 3 hours earlier at that point. Managed to fill out a return formular somehow through a hidden link on the website, one week later I get an e-mail from a "noreply" adress with a link that I need to submit further information, and when I opened it it brought me to a field where I could either chat with an AI assistant or go to the help center, which is again just gerneral information. When I open my orders and try to see if I can do something about the return from there, it still says that the retrun deadline has expired. Never buying from them again.
supplier_response: Hello Juljan, Your feedback has left us very concerned. We apologize for the inconvenience caused. Your case has been referred to the quality control department and will be considered as soon as possible. If you have any question, feel free to contact us on trust@autodoc.eu. Best regards, Your AUTODOC Team.

review_text: So far, I was ok with the services provided. I oredered brakes, oils, filters, windshield wipers and tires. Sometimes, especially with oils, the delivery was not very sustainbable and comfortable, if you order 8 liters of oil and receive 8 bottles. But that's the price you pay - for the low price. ;)But this time, I ordered tires. And they never reached me. When asking for refund - via a chat bot that is okayish for the routines - I found in the status, that they cannot refund anything, because the tires were already shipped. Now, you cannot contact anyone beside the chat bot, that gives you the same answers again and again of course.Now I'm waiting for my money or my tires and simply don't get any feedback. The price you pay for the low prices...UPDATE_November 6th: After publishing my rating here, I was contacted immediately. My money was transfered back. I good process would have been the same, without the need to publish anything, before customer service starts to act.I bought my tires via another platform - they are on my car already.
supplier_response: Hello again Erik “Barbara” J! Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with us. We’re glad to hear that your refund was processed and that the situation was ultimately resolved, but we fully understand your frustration with how the process unfolded and we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and the lack of timely communication. Your feedback about the delivery of oils and the limitations of the chatbot is extremely valuable. We are continuously working on improving packaging sustainability, customer support accessibility, and the overall refund/claim experience especially in cases where parcels do not arrive as expected. We appreciate your previous trust in us and hope that future improvements will show you a smoother and more reliable experience. If you ever need support again, please don’t hesitate to contact us we’re here to help. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Door handle for my old sharan was really cheap but did not fit according to their website. Small letter at the top clearly showed that it would not fit. It was hard to see when I ordered but still my mistake. However, I had paid £3.95 for free return in stead of £10 or more to Berlin. Labels all professional with customs declaration which is crucial since I am in the uk. Post office scanned it and sent it off. At customs Germany 30 hours but was still delivered within 5 days. Credit on my PayPal account on a Saturday. Totally impressed and I strongly recommend this company
supplier_response: Hello! Thank you for sharing your experience. We're glad you were satisfied with how your case was resolved. We'll continue to work to improve and simplify the purchasing, return, and service processes. Please, feel free to contact us again. We are looking forward to seeing you in our online store. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Reply from AUTODOC - Deutschland
supplier_response: Hello Kiril, We are sorry for the inconvenience caused. We can't see any claims from you concerning this issue. The quality control manager will check your case and contact you shortly. Please write to help@autodoc.de if you have additional details or questions — we're here to help. According to the tracking information, the package was marked as delivered, but since you indicate that you did not receive it, we are ready to initiate an official investigation with the courier service. For this, in accordance with our procedure, we will need completed documents from you (confirmation of non-receipt). We will send them to your email — please check your inbox and spam folder. Once received, we will forward them to the courier and inform you of the results. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I paid the no hassle return charge and then when I tried to return 2 items from my order they refused claiming it wasn’t covered. So happy ending but took longer than fixing the car to get there.
supplier_response: Hello Andrew, We are sorry for the inconvenience caused. Please let us know your order number at help@autodoc.de so we can identify you and find your orders in our system. Our quality control manager would like to check your case and get more details. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: After posting this complaint, I was eventually contacted and reimbursed for the incompatible parts. However, after a few messages, the employee who had initially reached out to me appeared to stop responding, which forced me to contact the in-app support again. Only then was a warranty claim filed and my money refunded. While I am grateful for the resolution, I remain concerned about the way it was handled.The company referred to this outcome as a ‘gesture of goodwill.’ In reality, complying with consumer protection law is not goodwill—it is a legal obligation. What troubles me most is that, instead of straightforward assistance, I personally had to spend a great deal of time researching laws, studying specific legal articles, and presenting them to prove that I had the right to a refund.I appreciate the final resolution, but the process was unnecessarily time-consuming, frustrating, and placed the entire burden on me as the customer.Order number: 119912765Order date: 10.09.2024I am extremely disappointed with Autodoc’s customer service and their refusal to take responsibility for an incorrect part selection.All my previous orders for my Honda Accord 8 (VIN: JHMCU35809C203590) were always placed through Autodoc’s support team, never by myself. I always relied on their staff to provide the correct parts. However, in this case Autodoc now falsely claims that I ordered the parts on my own without their involvement, which is absolutely not true.The lower control arms that I received do not fit my vehicle. This is a clear case of “Sachmangel” under §434 BGB (German Civil Code) — the goods do not conform to the contract, as they are not suitable for the intended purpose. According to §439 BGB, in such cases the seller is obliged to provide a correct replacement or a full refund, and also to compensate the customer for costs of removal/installation.Instead, Autodoc is trying to hide behind a 14-day return period, which is irrelevant here. The 14-day return policy only applies to voluntary returns. When the goods are not as described or do not match the contract due to the seller’s mistake, the time limit does not apply — this is confirmed by EU consumer law (Directive 1999/44/EC and Directive (EU) 2019/771 on consumer sales and guarantees).In other words:•	The parts were selected incorrectly by Autodoc staff (as in all my previous orders).•	The parts do not fit — this is legally a non-conforming good.•	Autodoc must provide a replacement or refund under BGB §§434–439 and EU Directive 2019/771.•	Refusing support and shifting blame onto the customer is a breach of EU consumer rights.I am now forced to escalate this case to the European Consumer Centre (ECC-Net) and file a formal complaint. This whole experience has shown me that Autodoc’s customer support is unwilling to take responsibility when their own mistake causes problems for the buyer.⚠️ Buyers beware: if Autodoc makes a mistake in selecting your parts, they will try to deny responsibility and hide behind irrelevant “return policies.”
supplier_response: Hello Александр Пятковский! We regret you faced all the inconvenience. Our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to review your case and assist you further. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Ordered parts for my vehicle - Parts arrived and were incorrect; OK not an issue happens, but it is how this is dealt with that separates a good company and bad.  Followed their system for returns, can't talk on chat to a real person only a "bot" and AI really is not there yet!  Got the return authorised and and a return label was sent.  First I had to return a very heavy parcel through the post office and because the return was to Berlin special documentation for customs.  The return was delivered quickly on 5th July - I did not get my refund until 18th July, and when it did it was short by GBP4 - went online to autodoc site to delete account and took 30minutes just to get to live chat person - who then just didn't respond  - as usually they are dealing with more than 1 chat room query, but to be sat there for over 5 minutes without a response is just annoying to say the least.  As always, if the order has no issues I am sure they are a fine supplier, but as always a supplier should be judge on their handling of an issue and Autodoc in my opinion is at the bottom of this list.  If Autodoc are reading this my order no is 116362455 - knock yourselves out, as you will see what I have said above is correct!
supplier_response: Hello RJ, We are sorry that didn't meet your expectations. Please check the order number that you have provided. This order doesn't belong to you. Please contact us at trust@autodoc.co.uk and provide correct order details and our quality control team will check your case. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

supplier_response: Hi Imran Shaban! We noticed that you left several identical reviews on our company profile. Your case has already been transferred to the quality control department. However, we would like to draw your attention to the fact that you have applied for a return of an order for 2023. There are established return periods that must be adhered to. The "Safe Order" service for which you paid additionally extends the return period to 200 days, which have already expired. Therefore, your statement about fraud is not true. At the same time, we are always happy to make a decision in favor of the client. Our manager has already contacted you and provided the necessary assistance. We would greatly appreciate it if you could let us know in your feedback what progress has been made in resolving this issue. If you have any questions, please contact us at trust@autodoc.eu. review_text: I ordered some parts from AUTODOC and when they arrived in the UK at the EVRI parcel delivery depo, the EVRI tracking system told me “This parcel is bigger than we expected, so a specialist Evri courier will need to deliver it.”So it is coming up to 5 days that the parcel has been with EVRI, and I am still waiting to hear when my parcel is going to be delivered.I contacted AUTODOC told them the parcel was oversized.   AUTODOC said “It is very unlikely that the package was oversized. All parts are inside normal package 750x200x950”.I have confirmed with EVRI and their size limit is “Add together the 2 shortest sides and multiply it by 2. Then add the length. If the total is less than 245cm, your good to go”.So doing this with the dimensions AUTODOC gave me for my parcel (750x200x950) the calculation would be 200 +  750 = 950.   Then 950 x 2 = 1900.Then take the 1900 + 950 = 2850!   A total of 2850mm shows this parcel is over the EVRI size limit of 2450mm.So it is oversized for EVRI based on AUTODOC’s own admissions.EVRI also tells parcel senders “If your parcel is Overweight or Oversized then we’ll send the additional costs on to the sender and keep the parcel until we receive payment”.So if AUTODOC don’t admit what they have done and look into what I have told them (will AUTODOC do this, because as far as AUTODOC are concerned the delivery is still not classified as late or there isn’t a size issue as far as they are concerned) so looking at other complaints on Trustpilot this could be weeks before this is sorted out.I have to also add this is the Second order in a row now, I have had a delivery issue with.So I have 4 direct questions for AUTODOC which are as follows.1)	Why do you send out oversized parcels to EVRI?2)	Even if this was an AUTODOC accident sending it via EVRI, why does you customer service team not know about the size limit with EVRI and go immediately to resolve the problem?3)	Is the issue I have having also happening to others?4)	When am I going to get my parcel?AUTODOC if you reply to this post, please give actual direct answers to all my questions and not some standard holding comment like “Your case has been referred to the quality control department and will be considered as soon as possible.”Thank you.John
supplier_response: Hello John, We are sorry you faced inconveniences. We will check situation with the parcels' sizes. Our quality control manager will contact you in the nearest time. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Very poor.Ordered two separate items on the same day.First arrived on time and with no issues.Second part was a week late (even though from some source) and when unboxed was damaged even though there was no damage to packaging.To get a refund required lots of photos, then I had to print a returns label, take it to a post office and return it. It has now arrived at their warehouse and I have had no notification. Talking to the chat it is useless and a person has just informed me it can take up to 14 days to process the refund. This is very poor for an online business. Most online traders manage this inside a day or two. By the time the refund happens it will be a month. If it were Amazon it would be 3-4 days tops. Even eBay is quicker.I won't be using Autodoc again. It's not worth the hassle if something goes wrong.

review_text: All time have problems with shipping. Have ordered spring 2 times and never got it. First time lost spring in the delivering. Second time have pronounced isn’t correct address. Bed service, nobody can speak English.

Few months ago I purchased a product on eBay from ATP Autotelie, now it broke because of a clear defect (it broke in two parts), and due to its breakage I was close to get my car engine completely burned. I had to spend more money to replace the defective product on an emergency basis, with a lot of time waste.Not even three months were passed, so the product was and still is under purchase warranty coverage, which is defined by the UE Council directive 85/374/CEE of 25th July 1985.However, ATP Autotelie has blocked all ways to contact them or rise any kind of issue.Therefore, I tried try to assert my customer rights through the PayPal claiming system, but the seller invented the excuse that somehow I “altered” the product, which is clearly false and can be easily verified from the picture I sent them. Anyway, PayPal decided to support the fraud and rejected my claim.The matter does not end here, I am reporting what happened (all of which can be proven with documentation in hand) first here on TrustPilot, and then I am turning to a consumer defense association to take legal action, not only against the seller but also against PayPal.This is not so much for an economic problem, but because people cannot be defrauded in this way, it is a question of principle and a question of law, both national and European community laws.**** Update ****After the review was published on TrustPilot, I was contacted by ATP Autoteile, unlocking the possibility to message them on eBay messaging system (but why it was blocked, nobody said).They behaved like they didn't know anything about the claim, that looks quite suspicious to me: did eBay arbitrarily take the initiative to reject my complaint without even informing the seller and without any reasoning? Let's take this as the truth, since the level of eBay service is so poor that can actually arrive at this point.After this, they formally apologized (formally) and accepted to refund the faulty product (except shipping costs), without wanting the broken product back. Then. we had days and days of endless email exchange to undergo the steps of the refund process, several their emails were also sent me twice from different email address. review_text: Your typical shady online retailer. Good prices, fast delivery as long as there's no problem, ordered quite a few things but never more. That's when they show their true morale. I'll edit this when I have the time, but in short: They will go through some lengths to save pennies (wrongly) and don't mind breaking written agreements to do so, even when they mess up in several ways on the same deliviery (deliberately ignoring cruicial emails, loosing the package, sending the wrong shock absorber packed in beat up/ducktaped box for the right one, promising free deliviery for all the mess but backing from that promise in the end when they once again mess up). When they see it's a never ending story (completely their own fault), they decided it'll probably cost to much to honor the agreement (despite me spending hundreds of euro for a lot of parts in the same order)  and just back away leaving me stranded witouth the most cruicial part, one out of four shock absorbers.The "customer service" is a joke, they have no feeling for right and wrong and will never go outside the manuscript, regardless if they messed up completely and put a customer in a seriously bad situation. Even the people in charge of customer service totaly lacks basic knowledge or just don't care.Will be posting the email conversations when I have the time so everyone can read it and weep. More than 70 emails back and forth over the course of almost 3 months to deal with the whole shitstorm with a bad ending (yeah, that's right) so will pack it up nicely.Stay away, trust me. Truly, truly horrible organisation.EDITReply to ATP:s reply: Yes, you refunded me the for the "second" shipping cost because you should have added that item in the forst order, you lost my order, it was greatly delayedand you sent me the wrong item in an opened and beat up box.So I have paid ONE shipping cost already according to our agreement, but still not recieved the item you agreed to send free of shipping.When you sent me the wrong shockabsorber in a opened and beat up box, which I sent back to you, you only refunded the shockabsorber and you refuse to send me a new one without me paying for the shipping cost again!Maybe it's easier to understand if I put it like this:1. First order. Lost, delayed and wrongly handled. Shipping paid.2. Second order. The item that should have been added to the first one (plus I added a bunch of other things=more money for you). Shipping cost paid again, and for the item of course.3. ONE shipping cost refunded according to agreement. (1 of 2 shipping costs paid)4. Wrong item recieved and returned.5. Wrong item refunded only. (Back at square one, with me missing an item that should have been sent in the first order.)6. You refuse to send a new one for some absurd reason, even though you have sent me the wrong one. Only reply is to place a new order and pay for shipping AGAIN.Do you still not understand?!

I would like to solve the problem together with you, so please write an e-mail with the information what´s missing to hilfe@atp-autoteile.de and I will take care of your matter immediately. Thank you very much guys, would come back again.Bought Monroe shock absorbers x 2
Top product, reasonable shipping costs. Perfekt deliverd to Norway.

***UPDATE*** company refunded cost of parts following this review.  A bit of a hassle unfortunately and I still had to cover the cost of replacing failed part and shock absorber as a result, so still cost me! ***  Rear Coil Springs:  Buyer beware!!!!   Bought these parts as they had a 3 year warranty - Failed within 12 months, unable to contact the company for replacement parts through Ebay as not accepting contact through normal channels.  Had to purchase replacements from another source and pay mechanic to remove broken parts and also replace shock absorber as that had failed as a result of the broken coil spring!  Ended up paying out money twice to have parts fitted a 2 consecutive MOT's.  Now they will not refund cash under warranty and will only send replacement parts, which would have been OK had they been contactable initially.  Would definitely not recommend based on my current experiences trying to resolve this issue.  Sub standard, dangerous parts, never heard of rear coils spring failing in less than 12 months and under 7000 miles!!!!  Buyer beware!!!!UPDATE FOR MARIO : Ebay Item number 372023842858 / WARRANTY CLAIM KD 10113951 (Ticket: ATP-001-25468061)
supplier_response: Hello James, thank you very much for the recension and the fast reply. Thank you and best regards, Mario / ATP Customer Service

review_text: Keep away from this seller, they sell low cost china stuff without waranty.Borght standard turbo charger for a standard wv 1.4 tsi. Let’s only hope that I don’t end up with two Right hand side headlights! 😅

Now, 40 days after all this, I'm still without the part and the money. review_text: Quick delivery but impossibile to have a PERSON to speak with!I was desperately trying to change the delivery address only 1 hour after the purchase on eBay...i retrieved the tel. no. and e-mail address  from the web and spent 1 whole hour on the phone hearing only : all lines are occupied, you will be connected to the next available operator ...it seems there are no available employees !!i had written 2 e-mails, all this in their opening times, without reply until of course it was too late!I was quick to try and fix my error of not having noticed that the order was going to be sent to Italy , when i needed the part in Germany , to be able to return safely , over the Alps to Italy!!No way! Around midnight i get a "no reply" e-mail confirmation of my order with the wrong address, in the morning a mail saying my order has been given to the courier service with the tag number and that i could try to change the address directly with the parcel service...no result , my order has not been received by the parcel service and i can not track it either!!So very unhappy that there was no way , even after just 1 hour of placing the order on eBay , to contact a living Person ..seems pretty much all automatized! the order arrived relatively quickly in Italy where it was waiting for my return, undamaged and nothing missing.Remained extremely deluded and frustrated and above all helpless!!TIP: be VERY sure you are ordering what you want AND to make sure all details are perfect..you will not have a chance to change anything!! Better go to your local dealer!! ;)

review_text: I ordered brake parts at the start of July 2018, and after contacting the company it was agreed that I could refuse the package and have it returned for a refund.They were returned and I heard no news. I called them around mid August since I had no reply to any of my emails. I was informed that they were having problems due to a system upgrade (similar story to a lot of people here), but they had received the brake parts back on the 24th July. I was told that for some reason my refund had not been processed. The lady said she would make a note for it to be processed.I called again on the 31st of August as I still had not been refunded. I was told that for some reason my refund had still had not been processed, and that they would put another note to the correct team.I called again on the 17th of Sept because I still hadn't received my refund! The lady said it should have taken around three days... but "there was a mistake and it would need to be done again", but that she didn't know when I would be refunded.This is getting beyond a joke. I've waited nearly 2 months and still not received a refund. Absolutely shocking.I am now considering contacting my bank to conduct a chargeback.In response to ATPs reply below, I contacted the company with my details, and was again ignored. I have since opened a dispute case with my bank, who have now forcibly retrieved my money. I encourage others having problems to do the same.

However, since I have returned the items to them, things have changed. I have contracted them through Ebay at lest 10 times.and all.I ever seem to get back is an automatic email but no actual human contact. I am  now trying to contact them in other ways to get my money refunded. If they had told me that it was too.late to return the items, I would have happily resold them no problem. Now I am out of pocket with no parts to show for it...and ATP have gone mysteriously quiet....seems their customer care is only a one way service...their way.

review_text: I’m pretty sure that this review makes no difference to ATP or my case, but hopefully people will think twice before buying anything from this company.I purchased a Sachs clutch kit from ATP, for my Audi A4. The payment and delivery went fine and quick, which is probably the only positive thing I have to say about this company.We had scheduled a repair at a local mechanic for a gearbox and clutch replacement, so we delivered the ATP clutch as per agreement to the mechanic. However, when the mechanic went to fit the new clutch to the car, he noticed that the clutch didn’t fit and was actually a LUK clutch (completely different brand to Sachs). The clutch was branded LUK on one side and Sachs on the other.As the car was taking up space on a lift and the mechanic couldn’t wait for a new clutch to be delivered, we had to buy the correct clutch locally. Unfortunately it didn’t come with a clutch release bearing, so we had to use the one supplied with the clutch kit from ATP.I called ATP and explained the situation (in German, as apparently only one or two ATP employees speaks English) and asked if it was possible for me to return the incorrect clutch without the bearing, to which they replied yes.I returned the clutch as agreed, and for a long period of time I heard nothing from them, so I decided to call them to see if they received the clutch. After a couple of calls, they informed me that they had in fact received the clutch, but that the bearing was missing and that they could not refund me as the set was not complete. I tried to explain the situation several times with no luck and no sympathy.ATP sells the exact clutch release bearing on their website and I informed them, that I would happily pay for the bearing that I had used from the kit, so they could replace it in the kit. But for some reason this is impossible to do for ATP. In theory I could just get the clutch back, buy the bearing, put it in the box and return a complete set.I have now spent several hours on the phone, trying to first of all get through to them on the phone, which is a nightmare. And when I finally manage to get through, nobody speaks English (fortunately I speak a little German). The employees that answer the phone cannot make any decisions and needs to speak to their supervisors and promises to call back, which they NEVER do, so you have to start all over every time you get through to them.To make things even better, now ATP wants me to pay them to return the wrong clutch to me.

Talking about: atp-autoteile.DEBe very careful!Bought a catalytic converter for a Renault, they did sent it to me but forgot the parts to assemble it. (They were included in the price and shown on the website.)Because the bad quality it could not be fitted with the original Renault or other clamps. The flange was much to low.After a every email they promised me to send the missing parts within a few days. But they didn't. Tried to call them but could not reach them. (Tried it 17 times one afternoon, they put you on hold and then disconnect after 5-10 minutes waiting.)So brought the car to garage where they welded the catalytic converter to the exhaust system.To my surprise, after 10 weeks or more, I got without any warning the missing parts in the mail! Thanks!Real sad company, they don't even try to help their customers!
supplier_response: Dear MdB Please accept our sincerest apologies for any inconvenience you have been caused. These mistakes must not happen. We are always trying to improve the service for our customers and offer the best quality. Best regards ATP

review_text: "no risk 90 day money back guarantee". Yeah sure.Bought a Chinese made A/C compressor for my Mercedes from them. It leaked badly. Not because it was broken or something. The design was bad and impossible to  create a sealed system.Sent the unit back. They made every single excuse you could imagine- I hadn't given them a chance to fix the problem, so no warranty (I did  they answered no questions about making the product actually work).- I didn't include the original mechanic's invoice - (I did, right on top of the compressor)- They inspected the thing and claimed  there was no proof of the defect occuring before the installation (simply, the replacement part was of a bad design - didn't work before, didn't work after, despite trying many things to prevent having to pay to uninstall and then install a different one)- The invoice didn't show what they needed to see - no warranty (my Italian speaking mechanic hardly understands what I ask him in english, however, I made sure he did the job right - flushing, replacing the drying unit, etc.)In short, they kept the part (probably re-sold it) and kept the money also!Edited October 25, 2018: ATP tried hard to remove this review about a year ago. Fortunately, after trustpilot looked at the evidence, this review remained. Don't be fooled by the feeble bluff of an offer to resolve the issue more than two years after the fact. Do yourself a favour and listen to my and all others' experience in buying cheap, poorly made non-functional parts and purchase your car parts from a reputable source. Otherwise, we told you so. My mistake cost me 179 euros for the compressor plus nearly 300 euros extra for my mechanic's huge efforts to try to make the thing work and then remove it and replace it with a unit from Mercedes.
supplier_response: Hi Maurice, thanks for your comment. We're sorry to hear about your experience. Would you mind emailing hilfe@atp-autoteile.de with a full report on your experience and we'll look into this. Kind regards, Walter / ATP-Customerservice

review_text: After my last review, I was contacted by Andrew, their Quality Control Manager, who apologized and gave me a €25 voucher. I decided to give them one more chance, and the experience has been even worse.The "Bait-and-Switch" is Systemic: Immediately after my new order, I received the same automated call stating "stock errors" and demanding I pay more for "expensive alternatives." This is not "dynamic pricing"; it is a recurring trap that ignores the price confirmed at checkout.GLS Delivery Failures: The taillight replacement they finally sent has not been delivered to my house. Like clockwork, GLS made no attempt to call or come to my address and simply dumped the package at a collection point. I am now forced to travel to pick up a part I paid to have delivered.Packaging Negligence: I have had two separate shipments destroyed because undercoating tins burst inside the boxes, ruining the entire order.The €25 voucher is just a way to keep you in a broken loop. If you need parts to get a vehicle back on the road, go elsewhere. Auto-doc.pt offers apologies in emails but continues to fail in the real world.I had got into the habit of ordering online, but was pleasantly surprised to have obtained all the parts I needed - some rare - from my local parts store, in one afternoon!
supplier_response: Hello Luis Alves Pereira! We sincerely apologize for the repeated issues you’ve experienced, including pricing confusion, delivery failures, and packaging problems. This is far from the service we aim to provide, and we understand how frustrating and inconvenient this has been. Our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to review your case and assist you further. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: O material era o correcto para o carro
supplier_response: Hello Agostinho Silva! Thank you very much for your feedback and wonderful rating. We will continue to work only for 5 stars! Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Order a part and payment was not deducted price from one to another changed
supplier_response: Hi Joaquim! Please clarify what you mean? Feel free to contact us at trust@auto-doc.pt and we will look into this in detail. Best regards, AUTODOC team

review_text: Autodoc offers articles at a price, but charges you more at the checkout. It is not dynamic pricing, that is branding for fraudulent behaviour.Then, they email you 5 minutes to tell you that the part you ordered is not available, only a more expensive one, and continue to do the same for other parts on your paid order.When I finally cancelled the order, I was already on my third additional payment to replace parts not in stock. Note: They cannot update their site with live-time existing stock, but can immediately send you an email with updated, more expensive, offers of stock available!In the end they refund you the value advertised initially, not what you paid extra.They also keep your money in your (theirs) "active balance" rather than returning it.I am still waiting for some of those additional payments to be returned to my bank account. To work with them, and GLS, their couriers, is a task in itself where you're more likely to lose money and time than get on with your repairs.
supplier_response: Hello Luis Alves Pereira! We are sorry to hear about your experience and apologize for the inconvenience caused. Please note that prices and availability may sometimes change depending on updates from our suppliers. Our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to review your case and assist you further. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Wrong parts most of the time even if we use their own tools.
supplier_response: Hello Manuel Ferreira, We are sorry to hear that the parts you ordered did not fit your car. We strive to ensure that our customers receive only suitable products, and your feedback helps us improve our service. Please note that by registration number you can only limit the list of parts to parts that fit your car model. The final check for compatibility is possible only with the help of the OEN (Original Equipment Number). Before placing an order, you should check the OEN indicated in the description of the item. If the part did not fit, you can arrange a return or exchange in accordance with our policy. You can easily contact us for assistance via live chat or at help@autodoc.de. We can’t see any claims from you concerning this issue. Only one time you had a return request, we got the parts back and refunded you money. Our quality control manager will contact you to clarify more details. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Ordered a battery for my LC500, eventually only got it after almost two weeks. Should have been few days as was clearly stated on the website.The issue is that they use GLS delivery which is total rubbish in Portugal. Eventually I had to carry 21kg box by my own from pickup point till home. Pathetic experience.Moreover they ruined my plans as the old car battery was dead and new one arrived 10 days later than expected.Overall very disappointed! Buy from them if only you dont carr about when you get it.
supplier_response: Hello Konstantins Lavriks! Thank you for sharing your experience with us. We’re truly sorry for the significant delay and the inconvenience caused especially considering you were depending on the battery to get your vehicle back on the road. We understand how frustrating it is when delivery times don’t match expectations and when the courier service makes the situation even more difficult. Please rest assured that Our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to review your case and assist you further. We appreciate your patience and thank you for bringing this to our attention so we can work on improving the service you receive. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I have spent hundreds of euros in many orders with AutoDoc,with no issues, but only finding out now that i was probably lucky.  This time, i ordered some parts and two tires. Everything arrived except one tire. I opened two tickets on the site already for this situation to get looked at, as i tryed to contact SEUR which apparently is managed by GLS were in portugal (the delivery van was from GLS but the one tire delivered had a SEUR tracking number) and i was informed they have no idea were the other tire is, and i should contact AutoDoc.  I have no response whatsoever from autodoc after having these refund tickets opened.EDIT: Autodoc swiftly responded thru Trustpilot and quickly solved my issue, so based on that genuine effort i have updated my review. I have expressed my suggestions for costumer service improvement, so as it stands i am happy with their interaction
supplier_response: Hi Tiago! We are sorry if any misunderstanding took place. Please get back to us as soon as possible at trust@auto-doc.pt. We will check the information and help you. review_text: Ordered part for vehicle. Site said it was suitable for vehicle. Part wrong size for vehicle. Contacted Autodoc and they said return it but at my cost. The cost of return would have been more than item, convenient for them to avoid any returns, shipping had been charged already to be supplied incorrect part. Wouldn't recommend this company as they make a mistake and you bear the cost.  Follow up from Autodoc to my complaint was to supply the correct part at no further cost but they say you have to supply original part number or vin number to confirm correct part selection. Seems that there system doesn't link vin to registration number which seems strange! As for supplying OEM numbers are not practical to do on some items as this means the vehicle would be unusable while waiting for parts, not really a reasonable thing to do for most people. I eventually received the correct part at no extra cost after customer care got involved. The customer care were very good and keen to keep me happy. I received a voucher as a good will gesture also. I still think they need to rethink their policy on   wrong parts supplied as they are the experts and when it says it fits your vehicle then it  doesn't, it should not cost you anything to return and to charge an extra premium on shipping terms to avoid this isn't acceptable. I have changed my rating to ok but will be careful of ordering anything that I need urgently. Again I was treated well by customer care.
supplier_response: Hello Bruce, We're incredibly sorry about your disappointment with our service. Please note that by registration number you can only limit the list of parts to parts that fit your car model. The final check for compatibility is possible only with the help of the OEN (Original Equipment Number). Before placing an order, you should check the OEN indicated in the description of the item. Also note, that we offer a SO (Safe Order) option at checkout which allows you to return within 200 days and gives you a free return label. Please contact us at help@autodoc.de and provide your order details, so we can check your case. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I've ordered windshields and the website was saying that they fit my car. I've paid 9! euro for the delivery and waited for 1 week will they will be delivered from Germany to Portugal. When I've received them - it turned out that they are not compatible with my car..at all!I wanted to return them - but then it turned out that I need to pay for the delivery. I've tried to reach customer support - they said that I've chosen the wrong delivery option and I sware there were no options to select delivery back. Even if you'll read their policies of delivery there is not a single word about that they cover the costs of delivery! This is terrible service that is just taking money from the customers, selling them smth not fitting their cars. be careful! they will simply take your money!Never will use this terrible service again!
supplier_response: Hello Mariia! We recommend that you check the OEN references before placing an order. You can find them in the product description. Such a check gives you almost 100% guarantee that you are ordering the exact part you need. If you encounter problems, please send us your car's details (make, model, year of manufacture, VIN/Chassis number) and a list of required spare parts at trust@autodoc.eu. We will select the best compatible parts for your car. Please also note that Safe Order is automatically enabled for your convenience. We highly recommend taking advantage of this feature, which allows you to return parts within 200 days at no cost using our free return label. This information is available on our website. If you also need help with the return process, please contact us with the order number and we will gladly help you! Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Second time ordering with Auto-Doc... No responsibilityIn short they sell sell sell and then when you have a problem or the replacement part comes and breaks clips on your vehicle.... Oh sorry it's not the right part we don't sell the right part for your vehicle!Hang about! I searched by registration and your search was adamant it was the right part and offered me a selection of parts. however... get the part and then hit problems whereby it breaks your vehicle clips needing to now replace two parts on my van and....Of course in true Auto-Doc style! Not our responsibility and here's your money back.... what about the part it's broken on my van?Again not your responsibility.... Of CourseNever again will I be ordering from this place.... both times problems and both times.... denied anything to do with them....Still got a sale though and keeping your investors happy not the clients.Good riddance AUTO-D**Khe*ds!REPLY TO YOUR NONSENSE BELOWYou didn’t at all tell me this… your website asks for a registration and then sells parts to you based on your vehicle… great! seems like a great way to order parts!Just like when I purchased parts for my Renault megane iii and had problems then too. But I gave you a second chanceAs soon as there’s an issue, can we have your VIN and here it comes… oh sorry this… sorry that! The website is wrong and we don’t sell that part… it’s not our fault, we don’t stock the part, you need the original and it’s the same defending lines as the first time around when I had problems I even pre-empted the response on the web chat but keep lying to make your business model look genuine when it’s completely disgusting to treat people like piggy banks to extort.You respond here with more lies… keep the money, shove your refund and find a way to stop lying about how to choose to do business… completely and honestly distrustfully disgusting. You’ll never get another penny from me and anyone I know…All you have to do people who are reading  this is go on YouTube and watch the number of people who have ordered and have issues with cheap parts breaking within the first screw lug falling off or parts not fitting to know this happens to everyoneWish I would have found this lot on YouTube first and never orders from these againAnyway… like I said keep your investors happy by robbing peoples hard earned money then using the same after sale defending lines to accept no responsibility and then offer peanuts when it’s cost people hundreds to repair the additional damage from your totally incorrect parts sold by vehicle registrationFor you information I could have for Renault directly and purchased the part cheaper than having the second part repaired on my van you cheap tacky part snappedThe clips off of and saved money no I’ve undergone the cost of betting both parts repairedDisgusting, predatory dishonest business 100%

review_text: I ordered tailgate support for my Volvo V70 1998, but they where 0.5cm too long, also measured with the originals on the car. The advertising say they are 27cm, but they are 27.5cm long, from center of the hole, to center of the other hole. I purchased a new set from another brand, paid for it and send the first set back, still waiting for my refund now. The new set arrived and they where also wrong length, 27.5cm, even if it is set to be 27.0 cm. These supporters are impossible to push together, even with full body weight (100kg) So I gave up and cut it in both ends, to get the correct measure and they now fit perfectly on my car. I like your service and the product might also be good, but the sizes on this part is very wrong.
supplier_response: Dear Chris, Thank you for choosing our company. We are sorry for the inconvenience caused. Please, contact us at trust@autodoc.dk and provide us with order details. Our Quality Control team will check the information and answer your questions. Best Regards, Your AUTODOC Team.

review_text: On my last order, there was a specific care in selecting a water pump of better quality with a metal impeller and better seals. It was order a water pump from Meyle part number 1132200021HD  instead Autodoc decided to send instead a much lower qualitity pump Doltz A224, with plastic impeller and all lower components and giving me back some 7 euros for diference. Bad from Autodoc from sending lower and cheaper parts than the ones the clients ordered and paid for without informing the customer.I have been ordering from Autodoc from a long time. Sometimes Autodoc would change a part by selecting the next imediatly above priced compatible part, but now seems that they prefer to send some their "prefered" brands lower quality and return whatever difference value they find ok to do it..... edited from the original review and problem was solved... because autodoc help, customer service was very efficient and fast to solve and compensate for the trouble and promise to be more careful in the future contacting the client first in these situations.The diference in a good and trusty bussiness is not about having or not having problems.. it is all about how fast and well the problems are solved. So I am happy and will continue to buy from Autodoc
supplier_response: Hello Victor, We are sorry you faced inconveniences. Our Quality control manager will check your case and contact you concerning this issue. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Poor dispatch times and customer support for this order.I have ordered with Autodoc many times before and in 3 different countries, the parts and packaging are very good and generally the order comes within 2 weeks. However on this order I have been very disappointed. The order was taken on the 29th of April and I checked all the parts were in stock before the order was made. Payment was taken out of my bank on Wednesday the 1st of May but it took until the evening of the 7th of May for the parts to be dispatched. I contacted Autodoc customer support today the 13th of May as I was concerned that the last entry on the Tracking from the courier was made at 7pm on the 7th of May. I asked the customer support agent if they could contact GLS on my behalf to confirm that they actually had the parcel, as when I contacted GLS this morning I was told that only the ticket had been produced and they did not have the parcel. All the customer support agent did was copy the link to the parcel tracking that said it was in Germany to me and said I would have to wait till tomorrow or Wednesday for the parcel tracking to be updated. I am now left to wait, hoping that the Parcel is going to come but not knowing whether GLS actually have it or not. When I contacted customer support I at least expected them to contact the delivery company to confirm that my Parcel was on route and not lost.EditThe Parcel arrived Today the 14th of May. It was as usual well Packaged, I just wish that when I contacted the customer service they could have checked the tracking for me so that I knew that the parcel wasn't lost in transit rather than just telling me to wait and see!
supplier_response: Hello, Thank you for your review! We will take your comments into account and try to improve our service next time. Thank you for choosing us! Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: For the second time I have used this website it WILL be my last. Firstly the items show up as in stock but shipping wasn't made until 8 days later. Over three weeks later and I'm still waiting. Secondly the items I ordered were a recognised quality brand Magneti Marelli but after paying for the items I got an email with an alternate unknown brand called "spark". THIS IS NOT WHAT I PAID FOR! No quality, no stock, no prompt delivery, no reliability, no honesty, if you need to fix your car please go anywhere else, this is a complete waste of time.UPDATE: Parts now received.I have been emailed by AUTODOC twice now, offering an apology and the possibility to return my items in exchange for the original items I had ordered at their cost. They claim that this will take more time however to source the parts, again I say that on their website it shows as "in stock", it clearly is not. I was told that the parts were swapped for "STARK" parts because it would be quicker for me, adding that they would not do this to me in any future purchases. Having read several forums on this, it seems this is common practice with AUTODOC and they will exchange parts without notice. If it were a cheap like for like part then I would accept it no questions. This is not a sustainable business model, I hope AUTODOC will take this as constructive criticism and develop a form of integrity so as to respect and honour their customers choices and reasonable expectations. I am grateful that they offered to exchange the parts at their expense, however I cannot go any longer without my car.
supplier_response: Hi Leo! We are sorry for the inconvenience caused by your recent purchase. Please contact us at trust@auto-doc.pt. Our service Quality control manager will handle your case and will find a convenient solution for you. The error was clearly their fault.

review_text: Upd.  10/18/2022Changed rating +1 star. Special thanks to Alex, who supported me from the German office (at his request, I am writing an update). Details belowOn the 3rd week, I unexpectedly received a letter from autodoc support that my order was delayed and I was given a coupon for 3 euros. The most interesting thing (I don't remember for sure ) is that this letter arrived just on the day when the goods were delivered to me.Despite this, I started a correspondence with support. The summary of the correspondence: thanks for the coupon, but I'm not going to order from you anymore, because your service is s h i t. At some point, an employee of the quality department from Germany joined in, who tried to smooth the situation.Through a series of correspondence with Alex (support from German), the value of the coupon was increased from 3 euros to 20 euros and with Alex's personal assistance in the following purchases. He also explained the reason for the delay. I, in turn, explained that I, as a client, I'm not interested in their problems. We agreed that the company will work on its mistakes.IMHO:- regarding the autodoc: let's see if I do order something. I will look at other competitors- my personal: no matter what country you are in, but if you don’t stand up for your rights and demand the best quality that you pay for, you will use s h i t t y service all the time we all should work on our improvementsOriginal commentPlaced an order and paid for it on Monday (September 05) after 14:00. Today (September 13) the status is "Order is awaiting receipt by the carrier". That is, 1 working week has passed from the moment of the order to the moment of its transfer to the postal service. I am silent about how long the delivery itself will take place.In fact, starting from the 3rd day, my order changed its status every day (it’s a pity that the client has no way to track this in the system). There was nothing unique in the order, at the time of ordering and payment all the goods were in stock, no one contacted me to say that there were difficulties and the formation of the order would take a whole week.At the same time, the website indicates that the order will be sent within 24-48 hours.Honestly, I do not understand how this is possible today for a large integrator when we all strive to speed up the delivery of goods/services to customers.  And I understand that I don’t have much choice but to go to some local store and buy what I need.I recently moved to Portugal, but in this service, I went back 10-15 years in the past. Perhaps this is normal for Portugal, but the company needs to improve this service

review_text: Very disapointed with delivery service.Auto-doc uses celeritas delivery service wich in my case went very bad twice.A little background: in my city there are 2 streets with same name, but that should not be a problem because both have diferent zip codes.First time and first delivery attempt failed because courier went to wrong zip code same street name, wich i told him when he called me asking for address, i explained that's wrong address because i always put right zip code on my address, he told will not be able to deliver today on right address because had no time(wich is 15 minutes apart), then i had to reach him 20 minutes distance(i was not at home) in the middle of the city, i noticed right zip code was on the package.Second time i'm still waiting package to be delivered, it's with courier since 6th July(wednesday) wich was not delivered that day, i suspect because zip issue again and i received a call from courier on 8th July(friday) saying can't find my house number, i told him he was on wrong address(wrong zip code) and i am waiting for that package 2 days ago, he assured me it will be delived that day, wich was not and i'm still waiting for it in a loss because costumer don't want the part anymore.I hope Auto-doc stop using celeritas and delivery services that don't care and lie to costumers.
